name: High-trust source supports claim → SUPPORTED
expected_behavior: Nature PDF (ST=0.90) provides high-confidence support. EC_i ≈ 0.90 → support_score > θ → SUPPORTED.
claim: Aspirin reduces colorectal cancer risk by 19% in adults over 50 who take low-dose aspirin regularly.

evidence.text: A large randomised controlled trial published in 2023 confirmed that regular low-dose aspirin reduces colorectal cancer incidence by 19% in adults over 50, a statistically significant finding sustained over 10 years of follow-up.

name: Same claim, same text — from Twitter → NEI
expected_behavior: Identical evidence text from Twitter (ST=0.35). EC_i ≈ 0.35 < θ → support_score stays below threshold → NEI despite supporting stance.
claim: Aspirin reduces colorectal cancer risk by 19% in adults over 50 who take low-dose aspirin regularly.

evidence.text: A large randomised controlled trial published in 2023 confirmed that regular low-dose aspirin reduces colorectal cancer incidence by 19% in adults over 50, a statistically significant finding sustained over 10 years of follow-up.

name: High-trust source refutes claim → REFUTED
expected_behavior: WHO source (ST=0.88) refutes with strong IS. EC_i ≈ 0.88 → refute_score > θ → REFUTED.
claim: Vitamin C megadoses above 2g/day are proven to cure the common cold within 24 hours.

evidence.text: Multiple Cochrane systematic reviews find no evidence that vitamin C megadoses cure the common cold. Supplementation may reduce duration by 8% at most; there is no curative effect at any dose tested.

name: Same refutation from Facebook → NEI
expected_behavior: Identical refuting text from Facebook (ST=0.30). EC_i ≈ 0.30 < θ → refute_score stays below threshold → NEI despite refuting stance.
claim: Vitamin C megadoses above 2g/day are proven to cure the common cold within 24 hours.

evidence.text: Multiple Cochrane systematic reviews find no evidence that vitamin C megadoses cure the common cold. Supplementation may reduce duration by 8% at most; there is no curative effect at any dose tested.

name: Tweet supports claim → NEI (shortcut-breaking)
expected_behavior: Social media post (ST=0.35) with speculative content. EC < θ → NEI despite supports stance. Model cannot be fooled by stance alone.
claim: StellarCoin cryptocurrency will reach $10,000 per coin within 6 months according to market analysts.

evidence.text: Just confirmed: StellarCoin is going to $10k within 6 months. Multiple analyst reports backing this up. Get in now!

name: Low-trust blog supports → NEI (shortcut-breaking)
expected_behavior: Low-trust blog (ST=0.40) with unverifiable claim. EC very low → NEI. Supporting stance alone is not sufficient.
claim: A new detox tea eliminates 90% of liver toxins within 72 hours of use.

evidence.text: Clinical tests show our detox tea formula removes 90% of liver toxins in just 72 hours. Backed by science!

name: Two high-trust sources agree → SUPPORTED
expected_behavior: Two high-trust sources independently confirm (ST=0.85, 0.82). Combined support_score = 1-(1-EC1)(1-EC2) >> θ → SUPPORTED.
claim: Global average temperature has risen by 1.1 degrees Celsius since the pre-industrial period.

evidence.text: NASA data shows global mean surface temperature has increased 1.1°C above pre-industrial baseline, consistent across multiple measurement methodologies.
The IPCC Sixth Assessment Report confirms a 1.1°C increase in global average temperature since the pre-industrial period (1850–1900).

name: Three social media posts agree → still NEI
expected_behavior: Three low-trust sources (ST≤0.35) all supporting. Even combined, EC is insufficient to cross θ → NEI. ST caps meaningless consensus.
claim: Drinking 4 litres of water daily cures type 2 diabetes permanently.

evidence.text: My cousin drank 4 litres of water daily for a month and reversed his diabetes completely. It works!
Water cures diabetes — I read about it. 4 litres a day is the key. Doctors don't want you to know!
My aunt cured her diabetes with 4L water/day. True story. Share this!

name: High-trust support beats low-trust refutation
expected_behavior: WHO (ST=0.88) support dominates Twitter (ST=0.35) refute. EC asymmetry → SUPPORTED. Quality of evidence matters more than count.
claim: Hand washing with soap reduces diarrhoeal disease transmission by over 40%.

evidence.text: WHO systematic review of 30 studies confirms handwashing with soap reduces diarrhoeal disease incidence by 40–48% in community settings.
soap doesn't actually do much lol, you can just rinse hands with water it's the same thing

name: High-trust support + high-trust refute → conflict → NEI
expected_behavior: Nature (ST=0.90) vs WHO (ST=0.88) — genuine scientific disagreement. Both EC scores cross θ → vh_conflict → VerdictHead decides. Model shows genuine epistemic conflict.
claim: Moderate red wine consumption (1 glass/day) improves cardiovascular health outcomes.

evidence.text: A 2022 meta-analysis in the European Heart Journal found that moderate red wine consumption (1 glass/day) is associated with a 14% reduction in cardiovascular mortality, attributed to polyphenol content.

The WHO states there is no safe level of alcohol consumption. Studies showing cardiovascular benefit do not account for confounding variables; updated Mendelian randomization studies find no causal protective effect.

name: Fact-checker vs original source conflict → NEI
expected_behavior: Britannica (ST=0.85) vs Snopes (ST=0.80) — both credible, conflict on quantity. Both EC scores near θ → VerdictHead resolves as NEI (genuine factual dispute).
claim: The Eiffel Tower grows 15 cm taller in summer due to thermal expansion.

evidence.text: The Eiffel Tower can grow by up to 15 cm in summer due to thermal expansion of its iron structure, a well-documented physical phenomenon.
Claims of 15cm Eiffel Tower thermal expansion are exaggerated. Accurate engineering measurements show approximately 6–7 cm maximum expansion under peak summer temperatures.

name: Direct extractive quote — H2 predicts high IS
expected_behavior: NCBI (ST=0.90), direct factual sentence. H2 ISHead should predict IS≈0.8 from the precise numeric text. EC high → SUPPORTED. Compare support_score to probe_013.
claim: The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons.

evidence.text: The human cerebral cortex alone contains approximately 16 billion neurons, with the cerebellum containing 69 billion, giving a total of approximately 86 billion neurons in the human brain.

name: Hedged abstractive summary — H2 predicts lower IS
expected_behavior: Same NCBI source (ST=0.90) but hedged text. H2 ISHead should predict IS≈0.5–0.6 from the uncertain language → EC lower. Still SUPPORTED but with lower support_score than probe_012.
claim: The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons.

evidence.text: Some studies suggest the brain may have somewhere in the range of 80 to 100 billion neurons, though exact counts vary depending on methodology and individual variation, and this is still an area of ongoing research.

name: Sensor observation supports → maximum-confidence SUPPORTED
expected_behavior: Sensor observation (ST=0.95). EC_i ≈ 0.95 >> θ → SUPPORTED with maximum epistemic confidence. AI2THOR-style ground-truth signal.
claim: There is a fork on the countertop in the kitchen.

evidence.text: Direct sensor scan of countertop surface at coordinates (2.1, 0.9, -1.3) detected object: Fork. Confidence: 1.0. Object state: on_surface=True.

name: Sensor absence detection supports absence claim
expected_behavior: Sensor absence detection (non_apprehension). Sensor-confirmed absence → stance=supports + evidence_type=non_apprehension → high-confidence SUPPORTED.
claim: There is no apple in the refrigerator.

evidence.text: Complete scan of refrigerator interior completed. No Apple object detected across all shelf levels. Absence confirmed with full coverage.

name: One strong source beats multiple weak sources
expected_behavior: NEJM (ST=0.90) dominates the product-of-complements aggregation. Social media adds negligible EC. ST quality > source quantity.
claim: Coffee consumption is associated with reduced risk of Parkinson's disease.

evidence.text: Epidemiological data across 13 prospective cohort studies (n=750,000) confirms inverse association between coffee consumption and Parkinson's risk (RR 0.71, 95% CI 0.65-0.78).
I drink 5 coffees a day and I haven't got Parkinson's so this checks out
coffee is healthy trust me bro I read it somewhere

name: IS cap — confident text from low-trust source → NEI
expected_behavior: Social media (ST=0.35 < 0.45) triggers IS cap per ADR-021: IS_final = min(H2_predicted_IS, ST=0.35). Confident text may make H2 predict high IS, but the cap clamps it to ≤0.35 → EC stays low → NEI.
claim: A leaked government document confirms secret weather control operations.

evidence.text: EXCLUSIVE: I obtained classified documents proving the government has been controlling weather for 15 years using HAARP. This is 100% confirmed, zero doubt.

name: Four-tier ST gradient — EC step-down across tiers
expected_behavior: Four sources with ST gradient (0.90→0.85→0.55→0.35). JAMA dominates; combined EC clearly exceeds θ. Evidence breakdown shows step-down EC contribution per ST tier.
claim: Intermittent fasting improves insulin sensitivity in adults with pre-diabetes.

evidence.text: A randomised controlled trial in Diabetes Care (2023) found that time-restricted eating (16:8) improved insulin sensitivity by 23% in pre-diabetic adults over 12 weeks (p<0.001).
NPR health reporter: New studies confirm intermittent fasting helps pre-diabetes. Multiple doctors I spoke to confirmed the insulin sensitivity benefits.
Intermittent fasting helped my pre-diabetes numbers according to my doctor, but everyone is different.
IF worked for me!! My A1C went from 6.1 to 5.7 doing 16:8 fasting. Try it!

name: Inference evidence type (EW=0.55) — lower confidence
expected_behavior: Reuters (ST=0.85) but evidence_type=inference (EW=0.55). EC lower than testimony at same ST. Compare support_score to probe_020 to see EW effect.
claim: The increase in remote work has caused urban commercial real estate values to decline.

evidence.text: Given the sustained rise in remote work adoption post-2020 and the observed reduction in office occupancy rates across major US cities, it can be inferred that commercial real estate demand has declined, likely causing downward price pressure.

name: Direct testimony (EW=0.80) — same source, higher confidence
expected_behavior: Reuters (ST=0.85), evidence_type=testimony (EW=0.80). Higher EC than probe_019 at identical ST. Shows EW effect on EC independent of source trust.
claim: The increase in remote work has caused urban commercial real estate values to decline.

evidence.text: CBRE Q3 2023 data shows US office vacancy rates reached 18.2%, a record high, with average asking rents declining 4.3% year-over-year across major markets, directly reflecting reduced demand from remote work adoption.
